title: Press Release | 655 million people still living without electricity underscore urgent need to deliver on universal energy access target
content:
JOINT PRESS RELEASE 655 million people still living without electricity underscore urgent need to deliver on universal energy access target Latest data show uneven progress, highlighting the critical need to focus on affordability and vulnerable communities   Washington, New York, Paris, Geneva, Abu Dhabi, 24 June 2026 – At a time when energy security and affordability have [...]

title: Ebola in DR Congo: first month of outbreak sees record number of cases – UN humanitarians
content:
Ebola has been spreading at unprecedented speed in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), bringing risk and fear into people’s daily lives, UN humanitarians said on Tuesday.  Read Full Story on UN News

title: Press Release | UN Public Service Awards to honor 12 initiatives for accelerating progress on the Sustainable Development Goals
content:
Press release UN Public Service Awards to honor 12 initiatives for accelerating progress on the Sustainable Development Goals Winning initiatives advance inclusivity, gender equality, transparency and participation in public service delivery New York, 22 June 2026 – Twelve initiatives from Azerbaijan, Brazil, Cambodia, the Dominican Republic, Indonesia, the Philippines, Saudi Arabia and Thailand will be recognized [...]

title: Powering resilience: How Indonesia is building a more secure energy future
content:
As countries grapple with energy price shocks, supply disruptions and the growing impacts of climate change, energy security has become a pressing concern.  Read Full Story on UN News

title: Global Ebola cases top 1,000 as UN races to reach DR Congo’s most vulnerable
content:
As global confirmed Ebola cases reach 1,000, nearly three million children and adolescents are at risk in the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while efforts increase to treat prisoners near the epicentre of the current outbreak, UN agencies warned on Monday. Read Full Story on UN News

title: Ebola in DR Congo: One month on, scaled up response remains insufficient
content:
One month after the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak was declared in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, case numbers continue to rise.  Read Full Story on UN News

title: DR Congo: Efforts ramp up as Ebola outbreak accelerates beyond borders
content:
The spread of Ebola is accelerating in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) amid continued armed violence, posing a grave and growing risk to the region, UN agencies warned on Friday. Read Full Story on UN News

The post DR Congo: Efforts ramp up as Ebola outbreak accelerates beyond borders appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Ebola treatment capacity expands in DR Congo as WHO issues new guidelines
content:
Treatment capacity for the rare and deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola is expanding in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), as the World Health Organization (WHO) warns that community mistrust remains a major challenge to ending the outbreak. Read Full Story on UN News

title: WHO and Brazil urge world leaders to finalise Pandemic Agreement to prevent future global health crises
content:
Global officials are calling on world leaders to finalise a crucial international agreement aimed at preventing future pandemics, according to a joint letter issued on Monday. Read Full Story on UN News

title: WHO commends Uganda’s Ebola response, urges vigilance and regional cooperation
content:
The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) has praised Uganda’s response to an Ebola outbreak that has spread from neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), while warning that continued vigilance and cross-border cooperation will be critical to stopping transmission. Read Full Story on UN News

title: Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children
content:
Drought, extreme heat and heatwaves are the most prevalent trio of hazards endangering millions of children globally, warned a newly released climate report by the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF). Read Full Story on UN News

The post Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.
